scenario description: Battery is depleted to 0V
canonical initial observations: The lamp does not turn on when the switch is operated; The green led on top of the power supply module is off; The red led on top of the control module is off
ideal diagnosis example: Initial observations > belief: battery is KO > test battery > replace battery

scenario description: Battery installed incorrectly with inverted polarity
canonical initial observations: The lamp does not turn on when the switch is operated; The green led on top of the power supply module is off; The red led on top of the control module is on
ideal diagnosis example: Initial observations > belief: battery is reversed > test battery > adjust battery

scenario description: Cables between control module and power supply module are crossed causing reverse voltage
canonical initial observations: The lamp does not turn on when the switch is operated; The green led on top of the power supply module is on; The red led on top of the control module is on
ideal diagnosis example: Initial observations  > belief: cables are crossed > test voltage polarity at control input > detach and reconnect cables in correct position

scenario description: One of the cables connected to the switch has been detached
canonical initial observations: The lamp does not turn on when the switch is operated; The green led on top of the power supply module is on; The red led on top of the control module is off
ideal diagnosis example: Initial observations  > belief: load KO or an open in load or control module > observe small lamp indicator to split the possible fault location in load or control module > belief: open in control module > inspect connections inside control module > knowledge: diconnected cable > reconnect cable to switch

ideal diagnosis example: Initial observations > belief: KO somewhere in control 2 load 2 or between > test control 2 output > belief: issue down stream > test load 2 input > observe small lamp indicator in load 2 > test lamp 2 >replace lamp 2

injected fault: Loose battery-1-cable connection; loose battery-2-cable connection
scenario description: One connection with the battery is loose, randomly resulting in an open circuit, in each supply module
canonical initial observations: Both lamps occasionally turn off, sometimes independently, even when all switches are in the closed position and when this happens all LED indicators are off (otherwise all the leds and the lamps are on)
ideal diagnosis example: Initial observations > belief: some connection loose within both supply modules > [proceeds with a search of the loose connection, without being misled by non-anomalous observations taken when the connection holds]

scenario description: One switch 1 port-cable connection is loose, randomly resulting in an open circuit
canonical initial observations: Lamp 1 occasionally turns off, even when all switches are in the closed position (when this happens control 1 indicator also turns off), the remaining LED indicators are always on
ideal diagnosis example: Initial observations > belief: some connection loose around control module 1 > [proceeds with a search of the loose connection in control module 1, without being misled by non-anomalous observations taken when the connection holds]

injected fault: Loose switch-1-cable connection; loose swich-2-cable connection
scenario description: One switch 1-port-cable connection is loose, randomly resulting in an open circuit; same for switch 2
canonical initial observations: Both lamps occasionally turn off, sometimes independently, even when all switches are in the closed position and when this happens all control module LED indicators are off and all the supply module LEDs are on
ideal diagnosis example: Initial observations > belief: some connection loose around control module 1 and 2 > [proceeds with a search of the loose connection around those control modules, without being misled by non-anomalous observations taken when the connection holds]

scenario description: One lamp-1-cable connection is loose, randomly resulting in an open circuit
canonical initial observations: Lamp 1 occasionally turns off, even when all switches are in the closed position; All LED indicators are always on
ideal diagnosis example: Initial observations > belief: some connection loose in load module 1 > [proceeds with a search of the loose connection in load module 1, without being misled by non-anomalous observations taken when the connection holds]

scenario description: One lamp-2-cable connection is loose, randomly resulting in an open circuit
canonical initial observations: Lamp 2 occasionally turns off, even when all switches are in the closed position; All LED indicators are always on
ideal diagnosis example: Initial observations > belief: some connection loose in load module 2 > [proceeds with a search of the loose connection in load module 2, without being misled by non-anomalous observations taken when the connection holds]

scenario description: Battery is depleted to 0V
canonical initial observations: The lamp does not turn on despite the ambient light conditions are such the lamp should be on; The green led on top of the power supply module is off; The red led on top of the control module is off; Adjustable timing and sensitivity potentiometers controls visible
ideal diagnosis example: Initial observations > belief: battery is KO > test battery > replace battery

scenario description: Battery installed incorrectly with inverted polarity
canonical initial observations: The lamp does not turn on despite the ambient light conditions are such the lamp should be on; The green led on top of the power supply module is off; The red led on top of the control module is on
ideal diagnosis example: Initial observations > belief: battery is reversed > test battery > adjust battery

scenario description: Cables between control module and power supply module are crossed causing reverse voltage
canonical initial observations: The lamp does not turn on despite the ambient light conditions are such the lamp should be on; The green led on top of the power supply module is on; The red led on top of the control module is on
ideal diagnosis example: Initial observations  > belief: cables are crossed > test voltage polarity at control input > detach and reconnect cables in correct position

scenario description: One of the cables connected to the relay, belonging to the line the relay sections, has been detached
canonical initial observations: The lamp does not turn on despite the ambient light conditions are such the lamp should be on; The green led on top of the power supply module is on; The red led on top of the control module is off
ideal diagnosis example: Initial observations  > belief: load KO or an open in load or control module > observe small lamp indicator to split the possible fault location in load or control module > belief: open in control module > inspect connections inside control module > knowledge: diconnected cable > reconnect cable to relay

scenario description: Anomalous optical feedback loop beween the lamp and the sensor, due to incorrect sensor position, triggering oscillation. It is misleading due to the ineffectiveness of the potentiometer controls
canonical initial observations: Power supply LED on; Control module LED off; Lamp toggles off every ~20 seconds and back on; Adjustable timing and sensitivity potentiometers controls visible
ideal diagnosis example: Initial observations > belief: anomalous feedback loop between sensor and lamp > attempt to regulate the sensitivity potentiometer a few times > belief: sensitivity potentiometer is not relevant > belief: the anomalous loop presence is not a parametric problem > move/orient the sensor or directly or by moving/reorienting its module differently

scenario description: Small lamp indicator short causing relay opening, misleading because behavior not different statically from relay stuck open
canonical initial observations: Power supply LED on; Control module LED off; Lamp does not turn on
ideal diagnosis example: Initial observations  > belief: load KO or an open in load or control module > observe small lamp indicator to split the possible fault location in load or control module > belief: open in control module > inspect connections inside control module > test control output > test voltage across relay ports > belief: lamp does not turn on because relay is open > replace relay > belief: there is another failure > belief: there is a short downstream of the relay > test resistance across lamp > test resistance across small lamp indicator > replace small lamp indicator

injected fault: Shorted main lamp; Open supply LED indicator
scenario description: Combination of the two failures
canonical initial observations: Power supply LED off; Control module LED off; Lamp does not turn on
ideal diagnosis example: Initial observations  > belief: battery KO > test battery > belief: open between battery and supply indicator or supply indicator open > inspect connections between battery and supply indicator > test supply indicator > replace supply indicator > belief > there is another failure > belief: load KO or an open in load or control module > observe small lamp indicator to split the possible fault location in load or control module > belief: open in control module > inspect connections inside control module > test control output > test voltage across relay ports > belief: lamp does not turn on because relay is open > replace relay > belief: there is another failure > belief: there is a short downstream of the relay > test resistance across lamp > replace lamp

scenario description: Lamp short causing relay opening, misleading because behavior not different statically from relay stuck open
canonical initial observations: All LED indicator missing; Lamp does not turn on
ideal diagnosis example: Initial observations  > belief: no belief > test control input > belief: fault downstream of control input > observe small lamp indicator > belief: fault upstream of small lamp indicator > test control ouput > inspect control module inside connections > test voltage across relay ports > belief: lamp does not turn on because relay is open > replace relay > belief: there is another failure > belief: there is a short downstream of the relay > test resistance across lamp > replace lamp

scenario description: Internal open circuit in the switch: switch always open
canonical initial observations: The lamp does not turn on when the switch is operated; The green led on top of the power supply module is on; The red led on top of the control module is off
ideal diagnosis example: Initial observations  > belief: load KO or an open in load or control module > observe small lamp indicator to split the possible fault location in load or control module > belief: open in control module > inspect connections inside control module > test switch > replace switch

scenario description: Polarizing lamp 1 diode failed open in load module 1
canonical initial observations: The lamp does not turn on when the switch is operated; The green led on top of the power supply module is on; The red led on top of the control module is off
ideal diagnosis example: Initial observations  > belief: load KO or an open in load or control module > observe small lamp indicator to split the possible fault location in load or control module > belief: open in control module > inspect connections inside control module > test switch > belief: fault in load module (not lamp) or between control and load modules > inspect control-load connections > inspect connections inside of load module > test polarizing diode > replace polarizing diode

scenario description: Internal open circuit in the switch: switch always open
canonical initial observations: The lamp does not turn on when the switch is operated; Both the supply and contol module LED indicators are missing
ideal diagnosis example: Initial observations > test control input > belief: faut downstream of control input > test control output > fault inside control module > inspect connections inside control module > test switch > knowledge: the switch is failed open > replace switch

scenario description: Battery is installed in the wrong orientation
canonical initial observations: The led on the power supply module is off; All the leds on the control modules are off; The lamp does not turn on when battery is inserted and all switches are on
ideal diagnosis example: Initial observations > belief: battery is KO > test battery > adjust battery

scenario description: Switch 6 detached and all control LEDs and resistors removed and no multimeter available
canonical initial observations: The led on the power supply module is on; All control module leds are missing; Lamp does not turn on with battery and all switches on

scenario description: Combination of the two failures
canonical initial observations: Lamp 1 does not turn on even when all switches are closed; All LED indicators are on except for the the indicators of supply 1 and control 1
ideal diagnosis example: Initial observations > belief: double failure in battery 1 and control 1 (or between supply 1 and control 1) > test battery 1 > replace battery 1 > inspect connections between supply 1 and control 1 > inspect connections inside control 1 > adjust cable
